updates.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 7 > Dinner > DIY Taco Night — Maple-Kissed Skillet

short_description.from: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.
short_description.to: Tender, slow-cooked beef brisket folded into corn tortillas with melted cheese and served with a rich, savory consommé for dipping.

meal_suggestion.from: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
meal_suggestion.to: Dip corn tortillas in the top layer of the birria broth before searing on a skillet. Fill with shredded beef and Oaxaca cheese. Serve with a small bowl of warm consommé topped with chopped onions and cilantro, and a side of lime wedges.

nutrition_note.from: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.
nutrition_note.to: A high-protein meal featuring slow-cooked beef. While higher in sodium and fats due to the traditional consommé and cheese, it provides significant iron and B vitamins. Serve with plenty of cilantro and onion to add fresh micronutrients.

updates.reasoning: Transformed the 'Maple-Kissed' taco skillet into Birria tacos as requested. Updated the name, description, ingredients, and nutrition profile to reflect the shift from a lean beef taco to a slow-cooked brisket style with dipping broth.
